# From Chapter 3 of the essayist's book (simulated)

Opening of chapter: "Every decision has a frame, and the frame is usually
invisible to the decider. The first move of any hard thinking is to surface
the frame."

Mid-chapter passage: "I asked a student once: 'What would change your mind
about this?' She said nothing would. That was the end of the conversation.
Not because she was stubborn, but because she had turned her position into
identity. Identity cannot be updated; only positions can."

Closing: "Two moves you can always make: ask what would change your mind,
and walk the exit path. If either answer is 'nothing' or 'no way out,' you
are in trap territory."

These three moves (frame-surfacing, falsification test, exit-walk) are the
essayist's framework.
